title: IIOS Capability Claims
description: The logical claims a capability asserts. This is the LOGICAL view only — it is not a wire format. Before signing, an issuer serializes an instance of this shape via RFC 8785 (JCS) to produce the JWS payload bytes. See docs/23_CAPABILITY_MODEL.md 'Issuance profile'. This object never appears on the wire on its own; the wire artifact is governance/schemas/capability-token.schema.json.

issuer.description: Identifies the Governance API instance/environment that issued this payload.

subject.description: Bound actor. Not transferable to another actor.

audience.description: The executor/service this capability is valid for. Single-valued for MVP (docs/ADR/ADR-0011-GOVERNANCE-MVP-OWNER-DECISIONS.md — audience). Prevents a capability issued for one execution surface being replayed against a different one.

resource.description: Concrete resource_ref. Exact match required at consumption.

policy_version.description: Policy this payload was evaluated and issued under; checked against the currently active policy_version at consumption, not only at issuance.

approval_id.description: Required (non-null) for any capability derived from a Class C action item.

not_before.description: Capability is not valid before this instant.

expires_at.description: MUST be short-lived and close to issued_at. Ceiling per class is fixed in docs/ADR/ADR-0011-GOVERNANCE-MVP-OWNER-DECISIONS.md — Capability TTLs by class.

max_uses.description: Intended use ceiling declared at issuance. The LIVE remaining-use count is server-side state keyed by capability_id, deliberately absent from this object — see docs/23_CAPABILITY_MODEL.md.

constraints.description: Additional scope-narrowing conditions the executor must satisfy, e.g. max amount, allowed commit SHA.

nonce.description: Unique per issuance. Combined with capability_id, detects replay of an already-consumed capability independent of the server-side use counter.

$comment: Deliberately absent by design, not by oversight: uses_remaining, revoked, revoked_at (server-side consumption state — see docs/23_CAPABILITY_MODEL.md 'What is NOT in the payload, and why'), and any signature-related property (alg, kid, signature — those belong to governance/schemas/capability-protected-header.schema.json and governance/schemas/capability-token.schema.json). No secret value is ever a claim here.
